PARISA WANG is looking for a remote Social Media Manager to lead social media planning, executionâ¦See this and similar jobs on LinkedIn. exec, social media United Kingdom, 2026-06-20T08:03:53+00:00 1133734 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-social-media-manager-luxury-fashion-tiktok-instagram-parisa-wang-1133734 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png Certified CMMC Assessor OCT Consulting, LLC <p></p><p>Certified CMMC Assessor (CCA)Â Â </p><p>OCT Consulting is a business management and technology consulting firm that supports Federal Government clients. We provide consulting services in the areas of Strategy, Process Improvement, Change Management, Program and Project Management, Acquisition/Procurement, and Information Technology.</p><p>Responsibilities and Duties</p><p>OCT currently has an opening for a Certified CMMC Assessor (CCA) to support the build-out of OCTâs CMMC Certified Third-Party Assessment Organization (C3PAO) practice. This position conducts CMMC Level 2 certification assessment activities as a member of the assessment team under the direction of the Lead Certified CMMC Assessor.</p><p>Day to day responsibilities include:</p><ul><li>Conduct CMMC Level 2 certification assessment activities as a member of the assessment team under the direction of the Lead Assessor (LCCA).</li><li>Examine documentation and artifacts, interview personnel, and test security controls across the 110 NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 requirements using NIST SP 800-171A methods.</li><li>Document findings and recommend MET / NOT MET / NOT APPLICABLE determinations for assigned requirements, with supporting evidence.</li><li>Support development of the assessment plan, scope validation, and pre-assessment readiness reviews.</li><li>Contribute to the assessment report and support POA&amp;M closeout activities within the required timeframe.</li><li>Maintain assessment evidence and working papers in accordance with C3PAO procedures and ISO/IEC 17020:2012.</li><li>Adhere to the Cyber AB Code of Professional Conduct, conflict-of-interest, ethics, and impartiality requirements at all times.</li></ul><p></p><ul><li>Must be a U.S. Citizen. U.S. citizenship is mandatory for this role because all personnel participating in the CMMC Level 2 certification assessment process must complete a Tier 3 background investigation resulting in a determination of national security eligibility.</li><li>Active Certified CMMC Assessor (CCA) certification in good standing.</li><li>Must be able to obtain and maintain a favorable Tier 3 background investigation resulting in a national security eligibility determination (this is not a security clearance and is not for the purpose of government employment). The investigation will involve a credit, fingerprint, and law enforcement agency check.</li><li>Bachelorâs degree in cybersecurity, information technology, information systems, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.</li><li>Typically 4+ years of cybersecurity or information assurance experience, including exposure to NIST SP 800-171 / CMMC.</li><li>Working knowledge of NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2, NIST SP 800-171A, 32 CFR Part 170, and DFARS 252.204-7012.</li><li>Additional certifications such as CompTIA Security+, CISSP, CISA, or CCP preferred.</li><li>Strong documentation, analytical, and communication skills.</li><li>Location / on-site: Remote-eligible with availability to travel to client sites for on-site assessment activities as required.</li></ul><p></p><p>Salary Range: $35- $50 hourly commensurate with experience, education, etc. This role may be available as either a part-time or full-time opportunity.</p><p>About OCT Consulting</p><p>OCT Consulting LLC is a SmallÂ Business (SB) providing professional services and information technology solutions to the Federal government and commercial clients. Founded in 2013, we bring the agility of operations and a management team with a track record of leading successful engagements at major Federal government agencies.<br /><br />At OCT we believe in creating a work environment where employees can thrive based on their abilities, skills, and achievements. We are dedicated to providing career growth and professional development based on individual merit and fostering a workplace where everyoneâs contributions are valued and recognized.</p><p><br /><br /></p> consulting, infosec, customer support McLean 2026-06-20T06:00:14+00:00 1133730 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-certified-cmmc-assessor-oct-consulting-llc-1133730 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png Growth Product Manager AltaML <h2>About the Role</h2><p>Most product managers learn about customers secondhand, through tickets, surveys, and sales calls. This role is different.</p><p>At Brilliant Harvest, our customers are heavy equipment dealerships. Their customers are farmers and construction equipment operators. Growth Product Manager owns the product surface that turns a newly signed dealer into an active, self-sufficient one and then into a reference customer. That means building the features, flows, and feedback loops that drive activation and retention, informed by being in the field with the people actually using the product.</p><p>This is a growth role, but customer success is in its DNA. You'll work closely with our CS team but your job is to take what that world surfaces and turn it into product decisions that scale.</p>\n<p></p><p><br></p><b>Key Responsibilities</b><p><strong>Own the Growth Loop</strong></p><ul><li><p>Define and own product growth metrics: activation rates, feature adoption, time-to-value, and retention across the dealer base.</p></li><li><p>Identify where dealers stall in the product journey and build solutions, whether that's a better onboarding flow, an in-app prompt, or a new feature that removes a blocker.</p></li><li><p>Design and run experiments to improve adoption. Prioritize ruthlessly based on what moves the metrics.</p></li><li><p>Work with Engineering, Design, and Technical Product Managers to ship growth features fast and learn from them.</p></li></ul><p><strong>Customer Understanding &amp; Feedback Loop</strong></p><ul><li><p>You genuinely understand customer success not as a concept, but as something you've lived. You've been in the field. You've sat with customers through the messy middle, after the deal is signed and before they're actually running on their own.</p></li><li><p>

newContent: Remote OK Assistant Store Manager Ross Stores, Inc. <strong>Our values start with our people, join a team that values you!<br><br></strong>We are the nationâs largest off-price retailer with over 2,200 stores, and a strong track record of success and growth. Our focus has always been bringing our customers a constant stream of high-quality brands and on-trend merchandise at extraordinary savings. All while providing a fun and exciting treasure hunt experience.<br><br><strong>As Part Of Our Team, You Will Experience<br><br></strong><ul><li>Success. Our winning team pursues excellence while learning and evolving</li><li>Career growth. We develop industry leading talent because Ross grows when our people grow</li><li>Teamwork. We work together to solve the hard problems and find the right solution</li><li>Our commitment to Diversity, Equality &amp; Inclusion, and our community. We celebrate the backgrounds, identities, and ideas of those who work and shop with us because our differences make us stronger. We strive to be a positive force in our community.<br><br></li></ul>Our Corporate headquarters are in Dublin, CA, we have 3 buying offices in key markets in New York City, Los Angeles, and Boston, and 8 distribution centers nationwide. With 2025 revenues of $22.8 billion, we are a Fortune 500 company who is committed to providing an inclusive work environment with continuous learning opportunities and development for our teams.<br><br><strong>General Purpose<br><br></strong>Responsible for the management and supervision of all areas assigned by the Store Manager and follows policies in regard to Customers, Associates and merchandising. Responsible for learning all phases of Store operations. In the absence of the Store Manager, the Assistant Manager is responsible for leading the entire operation of the Store to ensure that Company standards and best practices are consistently met.<br><br><strong>Essential Functions<br><br></strong><strong>General Operating Requirements:<br><br></strong><ul><li>Leads all Company Best Practices and maximizes productivity by minimizing steps and touches while working.</li><li>Assists in the analysis of Store reports to evaluate controllable expenses and overall Store performance.</li><li>Communicates any variances to Company standards to the Store Manager.</li><li>Ensures proper scheduling of Associates to meet business objectives.</li><li>Ensures compliance with all State, Local and Federal regulations.</li><li>Ensures scheduling and completion of all Associate cleaning tasks throughout the store, including restrooms.</li><li>Accepts special assignments as directed by Leadership.</li><li>Responsible for payroll administration including daily punch edits, Associate scheduling compliance, Sunday payroll transmission, and Payroll reporting as needed.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Organizational Development<br><br></strong><ul><li>Assists in recruiting, hiring, training and developing non-exempt Associates.</li><li>Ensures compliance of Ross personnel policies and procedures.</li><li>Assists with Associate Relations issues by communicating any incidents to the Store Manager or District Manager as needed.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Expense Control<br><br></strong><ul><li>Assists in the management of and continuous monitoring of actual expenditures to be within budget.</li><li>Controls payroll hours to plan, as the primary controllable expense, as well as adjusting to current business trends.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Maintaining a Safe &amp; Secure Environment<br><br></strong><ul><li>Understands that safety is the number one priority and practices safe behaviors in everything they do.</li><li>Ensures all Associates understand and can execute emergency operating procedures.</li><li>Maintains adherence to Company safety policies and ensures the safety of Associates and Customers.</li><li>Assists in the facilitation of monthly safety meetings.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Customer Service<br><br></strong><ul><li>Treats all Customers, Associates, and other leaders with respect.</li><li>Demonstrates courtesy, friendliness, and professionalism at all times. Recognizes Associates using Company recognition programs.</li><li>Executes Customer service programs and Merchandise Presentation programs through Associate training and program supervision.</li><li>Supervises and coaches Retail Associates in providing efficient and friendly service at the registers, Customer Service desk, fitting rooms, sales floor, etc.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Personal And Store Brand<br><br></strong><ul><li>Represents and supports the Company brand at all times.</li><li>Maintains and models a professional appearance, in accordance with the Company Dress Code. Reinforces the Company Dress Code at all times.</li><li>Manages Store to ensure a clean, neat, easy to shop environment.</li><li>Maintains a high standard of housekeeping with help of contracted maintenance personnel and Ross Associates.</li><li>Ensures scheduling and completion of all Associate cleaning tasks throughout the store, including restrooms.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Merchandise Processing and In-Store Marketing<br><br></strong><ul><li>Ensures recovery, sizing and markdowns are completed to Company standards through teaching, assigning tasks, and following up per Company best practice.</li><li>Ensures merchandise is presented and organized according to Company merchandising guidelines.</li><li>Urgently manages merchandise processing to the sales floor within the expected Company timeframe.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Loss Prevention<br><br></strong><ul><li>Assists with training Associates on Loss Prevention awareness and Store shortage goals.</li><li>As a representative of Ross Leadership, demonstrates integrity and honesty in all interactions with Associates and Customers. Safeguards confidential information, cash and credit card information and merchandise.</li><li>Understands and complies with the Loss Prevention Awareness Program and is responsible for Store Loss Prevention which includes maintaining a large Customer service presence on the sales floor, Customer and Associate engagement and heightened Loss Prevention awareness.</li><li>Assists in leading the annual inventory process including preparation and execution of inventory guidelines.</li><li>Monitors mark-out-of-stock policy to ensure proper administration.</li><li>Ensures Public View Monitor (PVM) system is maintained properly.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Competencies<br><br></strong><ul><li>Manages Work Processes</li><li>Business Acumen</li><li>Plans, Aligns &amp; Prioritizes</li><li>Builds Talent</li><li>Collaborates</li><li>Leading by Example</li><li>Communicates Effectively</li><li>Ensures Accountability &amp; Execution<br><br></li></ul><strong>Qualifications And Special Skills Required&l
